title: The weather voice
summary: A retired sailor reads the weather on a small Icelandic radio station every morning. A young intern asks why he still uses paper.

body.speaker: Aiko
body.text: Good morning, Mr Einar. May I sit and watch? I am the new intern from the university.

body.speaker: Einar
body.text: Sit. The broadcast is in seven minutes. Do not talk during it. After that, ask anything.

body.speaker: Aiko
body.text: Of course. Can I ask one thing first? Why do you read the weather from paper? The station has a screen.

body.speaker: Einar
body.text: I will tell you after. Watch first.

body.speaker: Aiko
body.text: [after the broadcast] You read very slowly. And you stopped twice.

body.speaker: Einar
body.text: I stopped because the sea wind was coming. The fishermen near the east coast need to hear that name twice. If I read it fast, they hear it once. Once is not enough at sea.

body.speaker: Einar
body.text: The screen gives me numbers. The paper, I have written by hand at four-thirty in the morning. I can see where I pressed harder. That tells me which warning matters most.

body.speaker: Aiko
body.text: That is a strange way to do it.

body.speaker: Einar
body.text: I was a fisherman for twenty-six years. I lost a friend in 1991 because a broadcaster read the wind speed too fast. The man was in a hurry. The wind was not.

body.speaker: Einar
body.text: Now you understand the paper. Tomorrow you can write the bulletin with me. We will write it by hand. We will not stop the screens. But we will use them as a check, not as a voice.

body.speaker: Einar
body.text: Four-fifteen. Bring warm tea. Bring a soft pencil.

annotations.meaningVi: ở ngoài biển — cụm cố định
tên hai lần / đọc tên hai lần

grammarNotes.title: Conjunctions of reason: 'because'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Because' nối hai mệnh đề để chỉ lý do. Trong bài: 'I stopped because the sea wind was coming.' (lý do tôi dừng lại). 'I lost a friend in 1991 because a broadcaster read the wind speed too fast.' (lý do tôi mất bạn). Sau 'because' luôn là một mệnh đề đầy đủ (chủ ngữ + động từ). So sánh: 'because of + danh từ' ('because of the wind').
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Because' joins two clauses to give a reason. From the dialogue: 'I stopped because the sea wind was coming.' (the reason for stopping). 'I lost a friend in 1991 because a broadcaster read the wind speed too fast.' After 'because' you always need a full clause (subject + verb). Compare with 'because of + noun' ('because of the wind').

translationVi:
Aiko: Chào ông Einar. Cháu ngồi xem được không ạ? Cháu là thực tập sinh mới từ trường đại học.
Einar: Ngồi đi. Bản tin còn bảy phút nữa. Trong lúc phát đừng nói. Sau đó hỏi gì cũng được.
Aiko: Dạ vâng. Cháu hỏi một câu trước được không? Sao ông đọc thời tiết từ giấy? Đài có màn hình mà.
Einar: Ta sẽ kể sau. Xem trước đã.
Aiko: [sau bản tin] Ông đọc rất chậm. Và ông dừng hai lần.
Einar: Ta dừng vì gió biển sắp đến. Các ngư dân vùng bờ đông cần nghe tên đó hai lần. Nếu đọc nhanh, họ chỉ nghe được một lần. Một lần là không đủ giữa biển.
Aiko: Còn tờ giấy thì sao?
Einar: Màn hình cho ta số liệu. Tờ giấy thì ta đã tự tay viết lúc bốn giờ rưỡi sáng. Ta nhìn thấy chỗ nào ta ấn mạnh hơn. Điều đó cho ta biết cảnh báo nào quan trọng nhất.
Aiko: Cách đó hơi lạ.
Einar: Ta từng là ngư dân hai mươi sáu năm. Năm 1991 ta mất một người bạn vì một phát thanh viên đọc tốc độ gió quá nhanh. Người đó đang vội. Gió thì không.
Aiko: Cháu xin lỗi.
Einar: Giờ cháu hiểu rồi vì sao có tờ giấy. Mai cháu sẽ viết bản tin cùng ta. Chúng ta sẽ viết tay. Không bỏ màn hình. Nhưng dùng nó để kiểm tra, không phải làm tiếng nói.
Aiko: Vâng ạ. Mấy giờ?
Einar: Bốn giờ mười lăm. Mang trà ấm. Mang một cây bút chì mềm.

options: The new intern from the university

questions.prompt: Why did Einar stop twice during the broadcast?
options: So that fishermen near the east coast could hear the name twice
questions.hint: Einar's reply about the sea wind.

questions.prompt: Why does the paper matter to Einar?
options: He can see where he pressed harder; that shows which warning matters most
It is cheaper than the screen
He keeps it as a souvenir

questions.prompt: How many years was Einar a fisherman?
questions.explanation: 'I was a fisherman for twenty-six years.'

options: He lost a friend because a broadcaster read the wind speed too fast
He moved to a new city

questions.prompt: What does 'The man was in a hurry. The wind was not.' mean?
options: The broadcaster rushed; the danger did not care about his schedule.
The wind moved slowly all day.
The wind blew toward the man.
questions.explanation: Contrast: hurry vs. patience of nature.

questions.prompt: What does Einar invite Aiko to do tomorrow?
options: Write the bulletin by hand with him
questions.explanation: 'You can write the bulletin with me.'

questions.prompt: Does he plan to throw away the screens?
options: No — they will be used as a check, not as a voice

questions.prompt: What time should Aiko come, and with what?
options: 4:15, with warm tea and a soft pencil

questions.prompt: What does Einar's pencil mark tell him?
options: What the weather is right now
questions.explanation: 'Where I pressed harder... tells me which warning matters most.'

options: Hand-made memory and a slow voice can save lives that fast modern systems may miss.
questions.hint: Notice why he refuses to drop the paper.

cloze.template:
Aiko: Good morning, Mr Einar. May I sit and watch? I am the new {{b1}} from the university.
Einar: Sit. The broadcast is in seven minutes. Do not talk during it. After that, ask anything.
Aiko: Of course. Can I ask one thing first? Why do you read the weather from {{b2}}? The station has a screen.
Einar: I will tell you after. Watch first.
Aiko: [after the broadcast] You read very {{b3}}. And you stopped twice.
Einar: I stopped {{b4}} the sea wind was coming. The fishermen near the east coast need to hear that name {{b5}}. If I read it fast, they hear it once. Once is not enough at sea.
Aiko: And the paper?
Einar: The screen gives me numbers. The paper, I have written by {{b6}} at four-thirty in the morning. I can see where I pressed {{b7}}. That tells me which warning matters most.
Aiko: That is a strange way to do it.
Einar: I was a fisherman for twenty-six years. I lost a friend in 1991 because a broadcaster read the wind speed too fast. The man was in a {{b8}}. The wind was not.
Aiko: I am sorry.
Einar: Now you understand the paper. Tomorrow you can write the bulletin with me. We will write it by hand. We will not stop the screens. But we will use them as a check, not as a {{b9}}.
Aiko: Yes, sir. What time?
Einar: Four-fifteen. Bring warm tea. Bring a soft {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: Reason: because the sea wind was coming.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Einar says, 'The man was in a hurry. The wind was not.' Where in your own work or life are you in a hurry while something underneath is not? What would it cost to slow your voice down by even one second?
